title: The MACU Report — Episode 9 — Perfectly Normal
notes: v2 punch-up (~5-6 min), loose/barely-simmering calm before the ep10 blowup. Fresh segments: MACU Markets + the Medical Desk (Dr. Bright LOOKS like a beaming TV doctor but is voiced by Herzog — bleak dread as warm health advice). Changes vs v1: Dr. Bright 'walk into the wind' detonates in Chip's weather (cross-segment but/therefore); Dusty's 'secrets are down' pays off when Walter casually admits selling a secret; 'heritage' escalates in the signoff; Dr. Bright buttoned ('sends a card every year'); Chip confident-idiot 'I laminated it this time'; cold-open 'everyone agreed to move on'; NEW deadpan post-signoff UNIT 7 SPONSOR card (implication-only creep). Arc held to ONE ignored Ron beat (c04 'feed humming'); moon de-escalated. All voices reused. Three new cards: macu_markets_card, medical_desk_card, unit7_sponsor_card. Reuses ep7 weather_card. subtitles.force_style in schema form.

comfyui.webp_to_frames: anim_dump -prefix frame_ -folder frames/<shot_id> clips/<shot_id>.webp  (ffmpeg libwebp demuxer chokes on ComfyUI webps)
comfyui.note: zeroscope_v2_576w (watermark-free). 384x384x24f locked — do not bump resolution.

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Run via comfyui.workflow with cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with vo!=null: POST {text: vo} to voice endpoint per speaker_map -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. Sequence shots in cue order; per-shot screen duration = its cue's VO wav duration / number of shots in that cue. Apply the analog-jank filtergraph per shot, RIFE 3x interpolate, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, burn subs (Better VCR font) from vo text -> final/ep9.mp4.

title_assets.macu_report_title: episodes/ep9/titles/macu_report_title.mp4 (auto-resolves to shared assets/titles if not staged)
title_assets.macu_report_bumper: episodes/ep9/titles/macu_report_bumper.mp4 (auto-resolves to shared assets/titles if not staged)
title_assets.macu_markets_card: episodes/ep9/titles/macu_markets_card.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes comp 'macu-markets-card'; Max: copy the latest render here)
title_assets.medical_desk_card: episodes/ep9/titles/medical_desk_card.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes comp 'medical-desk-card'; Max: copy the latest render here)
title_assets.unit7_sponsor_card: episodes/ep9/titles/unit7_sponsor_card.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes comp 'unit7-sponsor'; a flat, plain B&W sponsor card reading 'THIS BROADCAST SUPPORTED IN PART BY UNIT 7'. Deliberately mundane — the creep is the implication. Max: render + copy here.)
title_assets.weather_card: episodes/ep9/titles/weather_card.mp4 (REUSED — copy episodes/ep7/titles/weather_card.mp4 here, or auto-resolves from shared assets/titles if promoted)

beds.name: markets
beds.note: Frantic big-band bed under the chaotic MACU Markets trading floor (mid-roll, anchor='cues'). Loop the clip to cover the segment. Slightly hotter than ambient beds for trading-floor energy.

music.note: Soft AI big-band MACU Report theme. Per bed pick a random clip + offset; volume=gain, afade in/out, adelay to the bed's episode time; mix with VO via amix=normalize=0 (VO full, music under at gain).

subtitles.note: Better VCR burned-in subs. FontName/Fontsize NOT in force_style — stage 8 prepends fc-resolved FontName + Fontsize; a baked FontName would override the resolved family and drop subs to default.

ron.core: Will Ferrell as a 1970s television news anchor, thick brown mustache, side-parted brown hair, wide-lapel grey suit and tie, sitting at a vintage news desk reading the news, looking at camera

walter.core: Walter Cronkite, older television news anchor, balding grey hair, thin grey mustache, dark suit and tie, seated at a news desk in front of a bookshelf, reporting gravely

dusty.core: a frantic stock market reporter in shirtsleeves and a loosened tie, shouting and gesturing on a chaotic trading floor, a big board of numbers behind him, 1970s television

miss_cinder.core: an elegant composed woman in a tasteful apron and pearls, setting a table in a ruined concrete bunker, ash and rubble around her, soft domestic lighting, serene homemaker, faint smile

dr_bright.core: a smiling kindly doctor in a white coat with a round head mirror, cheerful reassuring bedside manner, standing on a 1970s television medical set, anatomical chart behind him

chip_pleasant.core: a manic grinning weatherman in a wide-lapel checkered blazer pointing at a chalkboard weather map, wild eyes, big toothy smile, 1970s television studio

broll.trading_floor: a chaotic 1970s stock exchange trading floor, men in shirtsleeves waving slips of paper, a big board of numbers, frantic motion, smoke
broll.medical_set: a 1970s television medical set, an anatomical chart and a plastic skeleton, an examination table, sterile lighting
broll.weather_map: a hand-drawn weather map of a wasteland region on a chalkboard, arrows and symbols

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU. I'm Ron, and it is, against all odds, another quiet night. No incidents. One near-incident, but it resolved itself in a direction we were not expecting, and everyone agreed to move on.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter. A full and pleasant broadcast ahead. Nothing ominous. For once.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Tonight: the markets, teeth are up four hundred percent, and the Medical Desk returns.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Is anyone else's feed humming tonight? That low hum, underneath everything? No? Just me. Anyway, to the money.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: We begin with the markets, where our correspondent Dusty Decker is standing by on the floor of the Exchange. Dusty?

cues.speaker: DUSTY DECKER
cues.vo: Thanks, Walter, it's a frenzy down here! Teeth are up four hundred percent on heavy demand from a vending machine I am not allowed to name. Secrets are down. Nobody's keeping them anymore.

cues.speaker: DUSTY DECKER
cues.vo: Batteries holding steady at one goat. And clean water hit a record high this morning. I want to stress that clean water remains completely unavailable, but the price is fantastic!

cues.speaker: DUSTY DECKER
cues.vo: In short: buy teeth, sell secrets, and do not, under any circumstances, look directly at the Exchange. Back to you!

cues.speaker: MISS CINDER
cues.vo: Hello, and welcome home. Company coming to the bunker this season? A few simple touches turn structural collapse into rustic. Drape a tarp. Light a ration tin. Call it a sconce.

cues.speaker: MISS CINDER
cues.vo: And if a guest asks what that smell is, smile, and say heritage. Cinder and Sage. Because the end of the world is no excuse to let yourself go.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And now, some good news for your health. It's the Medical Desk, with the always-reassuring Dr. Bright.

cues.speaker: DR. BRIGHT
cues.vo: Good evening. Drink eight glasses of water each day. You will still die. But you will die hydrated, which is something. It is not very much. But it is something.

cues.speaker: DR. BRIGHT
cues.vo: I am told teeth are a wise investment, so keep yours. The crater will take them regardless. And remember the old wisdom, an apple a day keeps nothing away. There are no apples. There is no away.

cues.speaker: DR. BRIGHT
cues.vo: For your mental wellness, I recommend a brisk daily walk, into the wind, until you can no longer see the settlement, and then a little farther. You will feel nothing. That is the goal. Stay well.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Thank you, Dr. Bright. He's very popular. He sends a card every year.

cues.speaker: CHIP PLEASANT
cues.vo: Thanks, Ron! And great news, MACU, after last night's unpleasantness, tonight's official condition rating is back to mild! We did it! I have my card again! I laminated it this time.

cues.speaker: CHIP PLEASANT
cues.vo: Light ashfall, a friendly breeze, and conditions are perfect for a brisk walk. Head out, keep going until you can't see the settlement, then a little farther. Dr. Bright recommends it, and honestly? So do I.

cues.speaker: CHIP PLEASANT
cues.vo: Oh, and the moon has returned to its usual distance. No follow-up questions. Anyway, back to you!

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: That's our broadcast, MACU, a perfectly normal night. Buy teeth, walk into the wind, and tell your guests the smell is heritage. If they don't believe you, Cinder and Sage has a forty-five-minute seminar on that. Sleep well.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Couldn't have said it better. Oh, I had a secret last week. Sold it. Got a fair price. Good night, MACU, stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: ANNOUNCER
cues.vo: This broadcast was supported in part by Unit Seven.
